summary: The functional test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because pip could not download a package from GitHub due to repeated 502 HTTP error responses from the server. The test was retried (marked RERUN) but still failed after multiple attempts.
root_cause: GitHub returned repeated 502 error responses when pip attempted to download `https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip`, causing the request to exhaust its retries.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Explicitly shows the failure was caused by repeated 502 responses from github.com, a transient server-side error.
The test was retried (RERUN) at least once before ultimately failing, indicating a flaky/transient network issue rather than a code defect.
The summary line confirms 3 reruns occurred in this test run, consistent with transient network failures triggering the rerunfailures plugin.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job; this is a transient GitHub 502 error. If it recurs frequently, consider adding retry logic or mocking the GitHub endpoint in this network test.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
